children.blurb: “Let all the forest’s nestlings.”—This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about a brother who drank water without straining it.
“A pleasing note.” This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about a Brother with many belongings. The incident is just the same as in the Devadhamma-jataka supra.
“While concord reigns.” This story was told by the Master while dwelling in the Banyan-grove near Kapilavatthu, about a squabble over a porter’s head-pad, as will be related in the Kunala-jataka.

“’Tis not the cold.”—This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about being seduced by the wife of one’s mundane life before joining the Brotherhood. Said the Master on this occasion, “Is it true, as I hear, Brother, that you are passion-tost?”
“With, wings that fly not.”—This story was told by the Master, whilst on an alms-pilgrimage through Magadha, about the going-out of a jungle fire.
“Ye denizens of air.”—This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about a Brother whose cell was burnt down.

children.translated_name: The Partridge, the Monkey, and the Elephant
children.blurb: “For they who honour age.”—This story was told by the Master whilst on his way to Savatthi, about the way in which the Elder Sariputta was kept out of a night’s lodging.
“Guile profits not.”—This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about a tailoring Brother.
“Methinks the gold.”—This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about a co-resident pupil of Sariputta.
“Far rather will I headlong plunge.”—This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about Anatha-pindika.
